title: silybum mariae
intro: Silybum mariae, also known as Milk Thistle, is a flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant's seeds contain silymarin, a complex mixture of flavonoids and other compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Silymarin is believed to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and hepatoprotective effects, making Milk Thistle a popular herbal remedy for liver and gallbladder issues, as well as for conditions such as jaundice, cirrhosis, and liver damage. Herbal preparations of Milk Thistle include capsules, tablets, and teas, which can be used to support liver health and promote overall well-being.
